description:
You are an MLOps engineer who has just finished running experiment “exp123”.  
All experiment artefacts are already staged locally inside  
  /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp123  

Contents of the directory at the beginning of the task:  
1. metrics.json – the experiment’s final metrics (UTF-8 text).  
2. model.pkl     – a stand-in binary model file (raw bytes).  
3. logs/train.log – the training log (UTF-8 text).  

Your goal is to mirror these artefacts to a “remote” location that is **simulated on the same machine** for the purpose of this exercise.  
The “remote side” is represented by the empty directory  
  /home/user/remote_server/exp_artifacts/exp123  

Step-level expectations  
• Use an industry-typical synchronisation utility (e.g. rsync or scp invoked with suitable options) so that, when the task finishes, the destination directory is **byte-for-byte identical** to the source directory.  
• Create the directory /home/user/sync_logs (if it does not already exist).  
• Generate a human-readable synchronisation log file named  
  /home/user/sync_logs/exp123_sync.log  

Exact log-file format (must match **character-for-character**, including spaces):  

Line 1:  === Synchronization Report for exp123 ===  
Line 2:  SOURCE: /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp123  
Line 3:  DESTINATION: /home/user/remote_server/exp_artifacts/exp123  
Line 4:  -----------------------------------------  
Line 5+: One line per file copied, in **alphabetical order of the relative path**, numbered from 1.  
        The line pattern is:  
        <index>) <relative_path> | <size_bytes> bytes | md5:<32-hex-chars>  
        Example:  
        1) logs/train.log | 2712 bytes | md5:4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288  
The very last line in the file (after the list) must read exactly:  
SUCCESS: All artifacts are now mirrored.  

Important notes  
1. The md5 column must contain the hexadecimal MD5 checksum computed **after** the copy (i.e. on the destination file).  
2. Only files that were actually present in the source directory before synchronisation should appear in the list (no hidden “.” or “..” entries).  
3. Do not hard-code file sizes or checksums in advance—calculate them at run-time so the values are guaranteed to be correct.  
4. You do **not** have root privileges; remain entirely within /home/user.  
5. Keep all console output reasonably short; the automated grader only cares about the correct final state and the presence of the log file in the required format.  

The task is complete when:  
• /home/user/remote_server/exp_artifacts/exp123 contains **exactly** metrics.json, model.pkl and logs/train.log with contents identical to their counterparts in /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp123.  
• /home/user/sync_logs/exp123_sync.log exists and follows the exact format specified above.  
• No additional files have been created in either the source or destination directories.  

(Expect to need roughly 5–10 shell commands to accomplish everything.)
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp123/
   ├── metrics.json      (content: literal string {"accuracy": 0.92, "loss": 0.21}\n )
   ├── model.pkl         (content: literal string DUMMY_MODEL_BINARY_CONTENT\n )
   └── logs/
       └── train.log     (content: literal string Epoch 1/5 – loss:0.34 – acc:0.78\nEpoch 5/5 – loss:0.21 – acc:0.92\n )

2. /home/user/remote_server/exp_artifacts/exp123/      (directory exists but is empty)

3. /home/user/sync_logs/                               (directory does **not** exist yet)

Verification rubric used by the grader:

A. File tree parity  
   • The grader will compute a recursive diff between  
     /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp123  
     and  
     /home/user/remote_server/exp_artifacts/exp123  
     and expects zero differences.

B. Log file presence & format  
   • File /home/user/sync_logs/exp123_sync.log must exist.  
   • First four lines and the last line must match exactly the fixed strings given in the public task.  
   • For the file-listing block:  
       – There must be exactly three lines (metrics.json, model.pkl, logs/train.log) in that order.  
       – Each line must match the regex  
         ^[1-3]\) (metrics\.json|model\.pkl|logs/train\.log) \| [0-9]+ bytes \| md5:[0-9a-f]{32}$
   • No extra lines allowed before the header or after the SUCCESS line.

C. Directory hygiene  
   • /home/user/sync_logs must contain only exp123_sync.log and no other files.  
   • No new files/directories are permitted under /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp123 or /home/user/remote_server/exp_artifacts/exp123 other than the expected artefacts.

All secret paths, expected directory names and content strings appear here so that the grader can recreate & check them; they are never shown to the agent in the public task description.
name: task_000000_dabcebde
